author: omerfirmak
content: memory traps from the logs I shared above dissapeared.

author: han0110
content:
I think the only requirement for the caller is ([ref](https://github.com/eth-act/zkvm-standards/blob/main/standards/c-interface-accelerators/zkvm_accelerators.h#L15-L18)):

- Caller MUST ensure all pointers are valid. If a function is called with a NULL pointer, the function SHOULD panic.
- The caller SHOULD allocate and free the input and output memory.

The implementation should validate the input, and ZisK does that I think.
